current_wind.description: Moderate southerly wind, gusts up to 10.5 mph.

wind_info.description: Moderate crosswind from the left, pushing the disc right.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, the wind is pushing from your left at 3 o'clock. Aim for a controlled RHBH with a hyzer to keep the disc from drifting too far right.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH is also a solid option here, allowing you to control the angle against the crosswind.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, for LHBH, the wind from your left and your natural fade will push the disc too far right. This is a challenging line. A controlled hyzer might work, but be wary of overcorrection.

fh.shot_strategy: A LHFH will give you more control against this crosswind and allow you to shape the shot towards the basket on the right.

wind_info.description: Moderate crosswind from the right, pushing left.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, this is a moderate crosswind from your right at 5 o'clock. A RHBH with a hyzer will help fight the leftward push.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH is also a good option to control the angle against this wind.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, for LHBH, your natural fade to the right works well with the wind pushing left. An understable disc thrown smoothly should ride the wind towards the basket.

fh.shot_strategy: A LHFH offers good control if you prefer that shot shape.

wind_info.description: Moderate crosswind from the right, pushing left.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, similar to hole 2, this is a moderate crosswind from your right at 5 o'clock. Focus on a RHBH with a hyzer to counter the leftward push.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH provides good control here.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, for LHBH, the wind from the right will help push your disc towards the basket. An understable disc thrown smoothly should be effective.

fh.shot_strategy: A LHFH is also a viable choice for control.

wind_info.description: Moderate tailwind.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, this is a moderate tailwind. Use an understable driver thrown smoothly for maximum distance.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH with an understable driver can also work, but watch for it to turn over too much.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, an understable disc is key here for distance in this tailwind. A smooth LHBH should get you there.

fh.shot_strategy: A LHFH with an understable disc is also an option, but be careful not to overpower it.

wind_info.description: Moderate headwind.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, you've got a moderate headwind. Choose an overstable driver for your RHBH to cut through it and prevent it from flipping up.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH with an overstable driver is also a reliable choice into this headwind.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, an overstable disc is essential for your LHBH into this headwind to prevent it from turning over.

fh.shot_strategy: A LHFH with an overstable driver will offer excellent control.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, this is a moderate crosswind from your right at 4 o'clock. A RHBH with a hyzer will help combat the leftward push.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH is also a good option for control.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, for LHBH, the wind from the right pushing left works with your natural fade. An understable disc thrown smoothly should carry to the basket.

fh.shot_strategy: A LHFH offers reliable control.

bh.shot_strategy: Player, another moderate crosswind from your right at 5 o'clock. Execute a RHBH with a hyzer to counteract the wind pushing left.

fh.shot_strategy: A RHFH provides a solid alternative for control.

bh.shot_strategy: Champ, the wind from the right pushing left pairs well with your natural LHBH fade. Use an understable disc for a smooth flight.

fh.shot_strategy: A LHFH is also a good option for accuracy.
